title: Neolloydia pseudomacrochele
intro: Neolloydia pseudomacrochele, also known as Devil's Head, is a cactus species native to Mexico, characterized by its vibrant, globular shape and short, stout spines. Traditional uses of Neolloydia pseudomacrochele include its potential health benefits, such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing pain and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, glycosides, and other compounds that have been shown to exhibit immunomodulatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective activities. In herbal medicine, Neolloydia pseudomacrochele is commonly prepared as a tincture or infusion, and is also used to make topical creams and salves, taking advantage of its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
taxonomy: Neolloydia pseudomacrochele is classified within the family of Euphorbiaceae and more specifically within the subfamily of Euphorbioideae. It is a member of the genus Neolloydia, a genus of succulents native to Mexico. The Neolloydia genus is further categorized within the subfamily Euphorbioideae, which contains approximately 60 genera. This subfamily is a part of the Euphorbiaceae family, a large and diverse family of flowering plants. The classification of Neolloydia pseudomacrochele reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the plant kingdom.
